mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and stimulates credit expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return following below-median FEDFUNDS levels is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and weakening aggregate demand, leading corporate earnings expectations to fall and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.4 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when the trigger fires during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales and payroll employment indicates a broad contraction in both spending and income, which amplifies downside risk to corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to produce a statistically negative coefficient (p > 0.05) in a pooled regression predicting 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects improving manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, which supports positive near-term equity returns when residential construction is not contracting.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of observations when INDPRO is rising and HOUST is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger real GDP growth translates directly into higher aggregate corporate revenues and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as expectations re-price.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth above its long-run median does not correlate with positive 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) during early-cycle recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation erodes real household purchasing power and raises the probability of tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 2.0 percent annualized over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of triggered inflation-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated unemployment alongside near-zero policy rates signals a liquidity trap environment where conventional monetary stimulus loses efficacy, depressing risk appetite and producing negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0 percent
failure_condition: The conditioned joint signal fails to produce negative average 5-day returns over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in housing starts points to weakening construction activity and forward-looking durable goods demand, which drags down industrial earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5 percent over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations following a 5 percent 3-month drop in HOUST.
